title: hieracium waldsteinii subsp. plumulosum
intro: Hieracium waldsteinii subsp. plumulosum, also known as the plumose hawk's beard, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its fern-like foliage and yellow dandelion-like flowers. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Hieracium waldsteinii subsp. plumulosum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial effects, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
